title: Magnum Opus
public_claim: A local scholarly retrieval and citation-governance system for research corpora.
distinctive_mechanism: It ingests a corpus, builds sparse and semantic indexes, exposes a query service, retrieves evidence, scores support, validates citations, runs critique or red-team checks, abstains when support is inadequate, and preserves source influence.
use_value: It is meant to be more than search: the point is to make corpus state, retrieval path, evidence scoring, contradiction, citation status, abstention, and answer authority inspectable on a local research machine.

flagship_proof_builds.name: Corpus admission and authority-control proof
flagship_proof_builds.claim: Magnum Opus begins before retrieval: it decides what enters the corpus and prevents duplicate files from masquerading as independent evidence.
flagship_proof_builds.input: one book or paper file with metadata, source path, edition signal, and duplicate-risk signal
flagship_proof_builds.operation: admit the source, assign identity, classify processing state, and record warnings
flagship_proof_builds.expected_output: source identity record with admitted / holding / failed / duplicate-risk state
flagship_proof_builds.verification_check: show one source moving from file path to corpus identity with state and duplicate warning visible

flagship_proof_builds.name: Hybrid retrieval and citation-support proof
flagship_proof_builds.claim: The system is meant to make retrieval path, evidence score, citation support, contradiction, and abstention inspectable.
flagship_proof_builds.input: one research query, source filters, BM25/dense candidates, and candidate answer
flagship_proof_builds.operation: retrieve, fuse, validate support, test contradiction, and narrow or abstain if evidence is inadequate
flagship_proof_builds.expected_output: ranked evidence packet, claim-support state, contradiction note, and qualified answer or refusal
flagship_proof_builds.verification_check: run one query and show retrieved passages, support labels, contradiction state, and answer boundary

component_proofs.claim_supported: decides what belongs in the scholarly corpus, what is stable but unprocessed, what is committed, and what remains excluded or failed
component_proofs.input: books, PDFs, Calibre records, manifests, root paths, source hashes, and status classes
component_proofs.expected_output: admitted corpus records plus holding, failed, and promotion states
component_proofs.current_evidence: Expected proof artifact: admitted corpus records plus holding, failed, and promotion states
component_proofs.verification_check: Run one corpus query or source-admission fixture through Root Registry And Corpus Admission; confirm source identity, retrieval path, support state, and output: admitted corpus records plus holding, failed....

component_proofs.claim_supported: moves from file identity toward work/edition/source identity so duplicate files do not masquerade as independent evidence
component_proofs.input: file paths, internal metadata, editions, duplicate candidates, OCR quality, genre signals, and source typology
component_proofs.expected_output: bibliographic identity record and authority-control warning surface
component_proofs.current_evidence: Expected proof artifact: bibliographic identity record and authority-control warning surface
component_proofs.verification_check: Run one corpus query or source-admission fixture through Bibliographic Identity And Authority Control; confirm source identity, retrieval path, support state, and output: bibliographic identity record and authority-control....

component_proofs.claim_supported: turns a book into structured scholarly records before retrieval or synthesis relies on it
component_proofs.input: admitted source, profile-only mode, structural detectors, bibliography/index/further-reading/glossary/acknowledgment miners, and optional argumentative extraction
component_proofs.expected_output: source profile, structural extraction database records, and argumentative-extraction candidates
component_proofs.current_evidence: Expected proof artifact: source profile, structural extraction database records, and argumentative-extraction candidates
component_proofs.verification_check: Run one corpus query or source-admission fixture through Extraction Pipeline; confirm source identity, retrieval path, support state, and output: source profile, structural extraction database....

component_proofs.claim_supported: uses hybrid dense/sparse retrieval with fusion and domain-aware constraints where available
component_proofs.input: committed corpus records, BM25 index, dense index, query intent, filters, and chunk windows
component_proofs.expected_output: ranked evidence candidates with provenance as a reviewable artifact
component_proofs.current_evidence: Expected proof artifact: ranked evidence candidates with provenance as a reviewable artifact
component_proofs.verification_check: Run one corpus query or source-admission fixture through Hybrid Retrieval Plane; confirm source identity, retrieval path, support state, and output: ranked evidence candidates with provenance....

component_proofs.claim_supported: breaks broad questions into evidence-searchable subclaims instead of asking one fluent answer from a black box
component_proofs.input: research question, subquestions, scope constraints, and retrieval results
component_proofs.expected_output: evidence packet organized around answerable claim units
component_proofs.current_evidence: Expected proof artifact: evidence packet organized around answerable claim units
component_proofs.verification_check: Run one corpus query or source-admission fixture through Query Decomposition And Evidence Search; confirm source identity, retrieval path, support state, and output: evidence packet organized around answerable claim units.

component_proofs.claim_supported: checks whether claims are supported, weakly supported, contradicted, or unsupported before synthesis is allowed to stand
component_proofs.input: candidate answer, retrieved passages, citations, and source lineage
component_proofs.expected_output: claim-to-evidence decision packet as a reviewable artifact
component_proofs.current_evidence: Expected proof artifact: claim-to-evidence decision packet as a reviewable artifact
component_proofs.verification_check: Run one corpus query or source-admission fixture through Citation Validation And Claim Support; confirm source identity, retrieval path, support state, and output: claim-to-evidence decision packet as a reviewable....

component_proofs.claim_supported: looks for source conflict, rival interpretations, and overconfident synthesis before release
component_proofs.input: supported claims, rival passages, source families, and uncertainty markers
component_proofs.expected_output: contradiction review and narrowed claim ceiling
component_proofs.current_evidence: Expected proof artifact: contradiction review and narrowed claim ceiling
component_proofs.verification_check: Run one corpus query or source-admission fixture through Contradiction And Red-Team Review; confirm source identity, retrieval path, support state, and output: contradiction review and narrowed claim ceiling.

component_proofs.claim_supported: refuses or narrows answers when the corpus cannot support the requested claim; the step records the decision path so the reader can see how the project moves from input to governed output.
component_proofs.input: evidence packet, source support state, contradiction state, and user query
component_proofs.expected_output: answer, qualified answer, follow-up search plan, or refusal
component_proofs.current_evidence: Expected proof artifact: answer, qualified answer, follow-up search plan, or refusal
component_proofs.verification_check: Run one corpus query or source-admission fixture through Abstention And Refusal Gate; confirm source identity, retrieval path, support state, and output: answer, qualified answer, follow-up search plan....

component_proofs.claim_supported: validates that an external package can be issued or shared without raw-text leakage, local-path exposure, or unsupported derived claims
component_proofs.input: redacted evidence package, share request, inventory state, operator intent, and retention rules
component_proofs.expected_output: share-authorized export package or blocked share decision
component_proofs.current_evidence: Expected proof artifact: share-authorized export package or blocked share decision
component_proofs.verification_check: Run one corpus query or source-admission fixture through Evidence Export And Permission Membrane; confirm source identity, retrieval path, support state, and output: share-authorized export package or blocked share....

component_proofs.claim_supported: keeps local evidence state and external sharing governed rather than treating retrieval output as freely portable
component_proofs.input: evidence states, local-state policies, share-grant requirements, negative-transfer exclusions, and retention rules
component_proofs.expected_output: state/share/retention proof record and review-ready audit surface
component_proofs.current_evidence: Expected proof artifact: state/share/retention proof record and review-ready audit surface
component_proofs.verification_check: Run one corpus query or source-admission fixture through State / Share / Retention Constitution; confirm source identity, retrieval path, support state, and output: state/share/retention proof record and review-ready....

minimum_public_demo: show one source moving from file path to corpus identity with state and duplicate warning visible
claim_ceiling: The page may claim proof-structured components and visible verification targets. It may not claim external validation, production deployment, or completed empirical findings unless the project-specific record states those facts.
